# Memory repo conventions

This is **yaswanth's personal knowledge base** at `~/memory/` (origin: `yash-learner/memory`).

## Trigger phrases — disambiguation

The word "memory" is overloaded. The user uses explicit phrases:

| Phrase | Means |
|---|---|
| **"save to memory repo"**, **"write a learning to memory repo"**, **"log this to memory repo"** | Write to **this repo** (`~/memory/`), as a markdown file. |
| **"save to memory tool"**, **"remember this in memory tool"** | Write to the agent's `/memories/` tool scope (Copilot's auto-loaded memory). |
| Bare **"memory"** with no qualifier | Ask for clarification. Don't guess. |

If the user says just "memory" while we're already actively editing files in `~/memory/`, it's safe to assume they mean the repo. Otherwise ask.

## Folder layout

```
~/memory/
├── README.md               ← top-level index
├── learnings/              ← topic-grouped lessons from real bugs/discoveries
│   ├── bash/
│   ├── css/
│   ├── git/
│   ├── react/
│   └── typescript/
├── logs/YYYY/MM/DD.md      ← daily journal entries
├── inbox/                  ← scratch / unsorted notes
├── snippets/               ← reusable code snippets
├── projects/               ← project-specific notes
├── .gitleaks.toml          ← pre-commit secret-scan config
├── .audit/                 ← (gitignored) hook output, do NOT edit
└── .git/hooks/pre-commit   ← runs gitleaks on staged content
```

## When the user says "save this as a learning"

1. **Group by topic** under `learnings/<topic>/<short-kebab-name>.md`. Reuse existing topic folders before creating new ones.
2. **File template:**
   ```markdown
   # <Title>

   **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
   **Context:** one-paragraph backstory — what we were doing when this came up.

   ## <Section 1>
   ...

   ## Lessons
   - bullet points of takeaways

   ## See also
   - [`../other-topic/related.md`](../other-topic/related.md)
   ```
3. **Cross-link** related entries.
4. **Stage + commit** with a message like `learnings/<topic>: <title>`.

## When the user says "log this for today"

Append (or create) `logs/YYYY/MM/DD.md`. Don't overwrite existing daily entries.

## Pre-commit hook awareness

Every commit runs `gitleaks` (see `learnings/git/pre-commit-hooks.md`). If a learning note contains an example secret pattern (e.g., a sample GitHub PAT, AWS key), **add the literal string to `[allowlist].regexes` in `.gitleaks.toml` in the same commit**, otherwise the hook will block.

Bypass exists (`--no-verify`) but should not be used here.

## What NOT to do

- Don't edit `.audit/` — generated by the hook
- Don't edit `Locale/` files (no such folder here, but generally locale files are managed externally)
- Don't commit real API keys / tokens / passwords — that's the whole point of the hook
